---
title: Session 01 - Introduction
number-sections: false
description: Open Science; p-hacking
---

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="Instructor notes"}
- Bring empty name tags and board markers.
- At the end, collect the name tags (and bring them along next time)
- Add the Moodle task where students submit their p-hacking results.
:::

## Overview

| Topic                               | Duration | Notes                                                                                                 |
| :---------------------------------- | :------: | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Introduction                        |    5     | Ankündigung: Zeitslot: 10:00 (s.t.) -13:00, aber bis 14:00 freihalten. Alle in Moodle eingeschrieben? |
| Empra Formalia                      |    25    | [Slides](../slides/Empra-Orga/Empra-Orga.qmd).                                                        |
| Students' introduction              |    15    |                                                                                                       |
| p-hack your way to scientific glory |    60    | [Slides](../slides/pdfs/Schoenbrodt_2024-p-hack_like_a_pro.pdf)                                       |
| Break                               |    15    |                                                                                                       |
| Introduction Open Science           |    60    | [Slides](../slides/pdfs/Schoenbrodt-2022-04_Open_Science_Primer.pdf)                                  |
| Homework: Practice p-hacking        |    5     |                                                                                                       |

: {.striped}


## Personal Introduction

- Your name
- Where do you originally come from, and where do you live now?
- Why did you choose psychology?

## Threats to credible research lecture

Join the live survey: <https://partici.fi/33173261>

![](img/Particify_Empra_1.png){width=300}

## Homework

- "individual" means: Everybody does the task on their own and uploads an individual report/result.
- "group" means: The working group submits a joint document.

### Homework 1 (individual)

**Practice *p*-hacking**: [https://www.shinyapps.org/apps/p-hacker/](https://www.shinyapps.org/apps/p-hacker/)

**Task:**
Hack at least 5 studies towards significance with $\alpha = .05$ (keep the true effect at 0).
Whenever you achieved a significant result, click on "Save" in the results table to store your result in the study stack on the right side. Once you achieved 5 significant studies, copy and paste the study stack into a text document. Keep a short log of the steps that were necessary to achieve significance (e.g., "increase sample size 2 times by 10 participants, controlled for SES").

Next, try the same for $\alpha = .005$ (if it takes too long, there's no need to achieve 5 significant studies).

**Deliverable**:
Copy both sets of significant studies (one for $\alpha = .05$, one for $\alpha = .005$) and your log into the textfield in the Moodle task.


### Homework 2 (individual)

- Create an [ORCID ID](https://orcid.org). You can use "Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München" as affiliation.
- Create an [OSF](https://osf.io) account (use your real name; link your ORCID ID).
